text.title: nodejs-service-layer-architecture Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for nodejs-service-layer-architecture as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Business logic in controllers: fat route handlers that perform validation, check for existing records, hash passwords, and create entities all inline.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Business logic in controllers cannot be unit-tested without spinning up HTTP infrastructure. When the same logic appears in multiple routes (create user, update user), it duplicates without a shared test surface. Move to service layer.

failure-mode.description: Direct Prisma access in services: service methods call prisma.user.findUnique() directly rather than through a repository interface.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Services that import PrismaClient are directly coupled to the ORM. Unit tests for services must either start a real database or mock PrismaClient, which is fragile. Repository interfaces provide a clean mock boundary.

failure-mode.description: PrismaClient instantiated inside a factory function or per-request handler rather than at module level.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each PrismaClient instance opens a new connection pool. A new instance per request exhausts database connections under concurrent load. The symptom is database connection errors that are intermittent and hard to reproduce in testing.

failure-mode.description: Inconsistent naming: UserRepository vs UsersRepository, userService vs UsersService, varying between singular and plural across entities in the same codebase.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Inconsistent naming forces developers to look up the exact class name before every import. Enforce a naming convention at project start: singular noun for all layers (UserRepository, UserService, UserController).

failure-mode.description: Async route handlers without try/catch + next(error). Unhandled promise rejections bypass the error handler middleware entirely, returning 500 with an empty body or crashing the process.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Express does not catch unhandled async errors automatically (Express 4.x). Every async route handler must wrap its body in try/catch and call next(error) in the catch. Express 5.x handles this natively, but most production apps are on 4.x.
